projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

needs_attention.title: Contact installers for quotes

insights.summary: The sustained presence of 11–14 connected devices during typical daytime hours, paired with zero camera activity, strongly indicates the camera system may be offline, malfunctioning, or intentionally disabled while the home remains active. This creates an unmonitored blind spot during a period when the family and pets are likely present.
High-activity pattern: living_room averaging 32 camera observations/day over the last 7 days.

The consistent presence of 12–14 devices with zero camera motion during typical daytime hours strongly suggests a gathering or stationary activity concentrated in a camera-blind zone (e.g., garage, basement, or backyard). A quick verification is recommended to confirm this is an expected visit and ensure the dogs are safe and accounted for.
High-activity pattern: living_room averaging 34 camera observations/day over the last 7 days.

The persistent high device count combined with zero camera motion during active daytime hours strongly suggests either a camera motion detection failure or a presence tracking anomaly; a quick system diagnostic is recommended.

The combination of consistently high device presence (12-14) with zero camera motion over a 4-hour midday window is highly atypical for a family with children and active dogs, strongly suggesting either a camera system outage/misconfiguration or an unusually static household state. This matters because it could mask security events or indicate a network issue that requires verification.
High-activity pattern: front_yard averaging 22 camera observations/day over the last 7 days.

The steady decline in connected devices (18 to 11) paired with zero camera activity on a Thursday morning confirms the household is executing their normal departure routine, likely via the garage or side entrance. This cross-signal pattern validates the morning departure and indicates no security anomaly, with the slight device count increase at 09:14 likely being a delayed Wi-Fi reconnection or a smart device coming online.
High-activity pattern: entryway averaging 23 camera observations/day over the last 7 days.

The steady decline in connected devices (20 to 12) combined with zero camera triggers indicates the family is likely departing for school and work through an unmonitored path or before cameras were armed. This aligns with a typical Thursday morning routine, but highlights a minor gap in departure verification that Steve may want to address.
